nativeRange.habitat: Sheltered coral reefs with rich coral growth at depths of roughly 5–30 m; tends to congregate in small groups among branching corals and rocky crevices

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a varied carnivorous diet in captivity, including mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty foods (krill, shrimp, squid, clam, mussel), and most quality marine flake or pellet preparations. Generally advised to feed 2–3 small meals per day.

summary: A small, vivid yellow-and-purple Western Pacific dottyback that is generally considered beginner-friendly and reef-safe. Best kept singly; tends to be territorial and has been known to eat ornamental shrimp.
careNotes:
The Purple Stripe Dottyback (also widely traded under the older synonym Pseudochromis diadema) is generally considered one of the more straightforward saltwater fish for new keepers — it is hardy, eats readily, and tolerates a typical reef-tank parameter window. It tends to spend its time darting in and out of rockwork in the mid water column, so plenty of caves, overhangs, and branching structure are generally advised.

Temperament is a genuine point of disagreement across sources. LiveAquaria and BlueZoo Aquatics describe the species as aggressive and territorial, especially toward similarly shaped or similarly colored tankmates. Reefarchive characterizes it as the most peaceful dottyback and community-tolerant. The semi-aggressive rating used here reflects the middle of that spread. In practice it tends to defend a small home territory in the rockwork and have been known to bully smaller, more timid fish; pair it with moderately robust tankmates such as tangs, larger damselfish, hawkfish, or angelfish rather than with shy gobies or anthias.

Conspecific aggression is reported as high — keep one per tank unless the system is unusually large with multiple discrete territories. Reef compatibility is generally considered good with corals and sessile invertebrates, but the species has been known to pick at small ornamental shrimp and other mobile micro-inverts, so a true shrimp-and-fish reef is not the safest pairing.

Cross-source spread: adult size — FishBase reports 6.2 cm TL, BlueZoo 2.5 in (6.4 cm), LiveAquaria 3 in (7.6 cm), Reefarchive 2.4 in (6.1 cm); averaged max ~7 cm. Temperature — LiveAquaria and BlueZoo state 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); Reefarchive 72–80°F (22.2–26.7°C); averaged max 26.1°C. Lifespan — one trade source lists ~4 years while Reefarchive lists 5–7 years; range broadened accordingly. Minimum tank size is unanimous at 30 US gal (~114 L); rounded to 120 L for a nicer aquarium-standard value, with a 75 cm footprint to give the territorial behavior room to play out.

Nomenclature note: the species was reclassified from Pseudochromis to Pictichromis (Gill, Pyle & Earle, 2009) and FishBase now lists Pictichromis diadema as the accepted name. Most retail listings still use the older Pseudochromis diadema synonym.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to spawn in captivity. The mature male typically prepares a cave or crevice and courts a female who deposits a small egg ball inside the prepared site. Captive-bred specimens are increasingly available in the trade and tend to be more even-tempered than wild-caught stock. Raising fry is generally considered moderately challenging due to the small size of the larvae.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Used for minimum tank size (30 gal), water parameters (temp 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), care level (Easy), temperament (Aggressive), reef-safe (Yes), diet (carnivore — meaty foods), max size (3 in), and the caveat about eating ornamental shrimp. Note: LiveAquaria still lists the older synonym Pseudochromis diadema.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked: scientific name (Pseudochromis diadema synonym), 30 gal minimum, 2.5 in adult size, aggressive temperament, beginner care level, carnivore diet, reef-safe.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for confirmed accepted scientific name Pictichromis diadema, family Pseudochromidae, order Ovalentaria, max length 6.2 cm TL, depth range 5–30 m, native distribution (Philippines, Peninsular Malaysia, northern Borneo), reef-associated habitat.

additional.name: Reefarchive
additional.notes: Third source for water parameters (72–80°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.026, dKH 8–12), care level (beginner), reef-safe (yes), lifespan (5–7 years), and conflicting temperament report (peaceful) used to balance the LiveAquaria/BlueZoo aggressive rating to semi-aggressive. Adult size 2.4 in (6.1 cm).

fish.breedingNotes: Cave-spawning species; male prepares a hiding site and courts the female, who deposits a small egg ball inside the cave. Captive-bred specimens are increasingly available and tend to be more even-tempered than wild-caught fish. Raising fry is considered moderately challenging.
